notes: Released September 1997; fully rewrote to exploit Windows APIs; later became IBM Tivoli NetView; still lives as IBM Tivoli NetView for z/OS in mainframe form
HP NNM 5.0 for NT; earlier versions dated to early 1990s; version 6.4 discontinued December 2007; version 7.5x discontinued May 2010; succeeded by HP NNMi then OpenText NNM
Framework integrating systems and network management; TME 10 was the 10th-generation unified platform
Primary deployment target for both NetView and NNM in this study; succeeded by Windows 2000 Server
Used for web-based remote management in NetView
Tivoli TME 10 NetView was first network management product to adopt DHCP for dynamic IP management; reduces false alarms
Standard protocol for network device management; foundation for both NNM and NetView
TME 10 NetView web browser used Java for dynamic interface
HP NNM web browser used static HTML only; NetView used Java+JavaScript+HTML
HP NNM required data export to MS Excel for custom queries; Tivoli NetView provided built-in reporting
Original platform for IBM NetView; TME 10 NetView 5.0 also ran on AIX
Supported by both TME 10 NetView and HP NNM
Original platform for HP OpenView/NNM; HP-UX support ended 2025
Supported by TME 10 NetView; DEC acquired by Compaq 1998 then HP 2002
